author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
title: The Effect of Divine Grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Chapter 13 [VIII.]—The Effect of Divine Grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Accordingly, our only Master and Lord Himself, when He had said what I have above mentioned,—“This is the work of God, that ye believe on Him whom He hath sent,”—says a little afterwards in that same discourse of His, “I said unto you that ye also have seen me and have not believed.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What is the meaning of “shall come to me,” but, “shall believe in me”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But it is the Father’s gift that this may be the case.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, a little after He says, “Murmur not among yourselves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: No one can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him; and I will raise him up at the last day.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all teachable of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Every man that hath heard of the Father, and hath learned, cometh unto me.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: What is the meaning of, “Every man that hath heard from the Father, and hath learned, cometh unto me,” except that there is none who hears from the Father, and learns, who cometh not to me?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For if every one who has heard from the Father, and has learned, comes, certainly every one who does not come has not heard from the Father; for if he had heard and learned, he would come.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For no one has heard and learned, and has not come; but every one, as the Truth declares, who has heard from the Father, and has learned, comes.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Far removed from the senses of the flesh is this teaching in which the Father is heard, and teaches to come to the Son.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Engaged herein is also the Son Himself, because He is His Word by which He thus teaches; and He does not do this through the ear of the flesh, but of the heart.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Herein engaged, also, at the same time, is the Spirit of the Father and of the Son; and He, too, teaches, and does not teach separately, since we have learned that the workings of the Trinity are inseparable.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And that is certainly the same Holy Spirit of whom the apostle says, “We, however, having the same Spirit of faith.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But this is especially attributed to the Father, for the reason that of Him is begotten the Only Begotten, and from Him proceeds the Holy Spirit, of which it would be tedious to argue more elaborately; and I think that my work in fifteen books on the Trinity which God is, has already reached you.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Very far removed, I say, from the senses of the flesh is this instruction wherein God is heard and teaches.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: We see that many come to the Son because we see that many believe on Christ, but when and how they have heard this from the Father, and have learned, we see not.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It is true that that grace is exceedingly secret, but who doubts that it is grace?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This grace, therefore, which is hiddenly bestowed in human hearts by the Divine gift, is rejected by no hard heart, because it is given for the sake of first taking away the hardness of the heart.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: When, therefore, the Father is heard within, and teaches, so that a man comes to the Son, He takes away the heart of stone and gives a heart of flesh, as in the declaration of the prophet He has promised.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because He thus makes them children and vessels of mercy which He has prepared for glory.
